heading: Shared savings program
label: 1395jjj Shared savings program

children.heading: In general1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
children.label: 1 In general1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
A
children.text: groups of providers of services and suppliers meeting criteria specified by the Secretary may work together to manage and coordinate care for Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries through an accountable care organization (referred to in this section as an “ACO”); and

children.label: B
children.text: ACOs that meet quality performance standards established by the Secretary are eligible to receive payments for shared savings under subsection (d)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: ACO professionals in group practice arrangements.

children.label: B
children.text: Networks of individual practices of ACO professionals.

children.label: C
children.text: Partnerships or joint venture arrangements between hospitals and ACO professionals.

children.label: D
children.text: Hospitals employing ACO professionals.

children.label: E
children.text: Such other groups of providers of services and suppliers as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: The ACO shall be willing to become accountable for the quality, cost, and overall care of the Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries assigned to it.

children.label: B
children.text: The ACO shall enter into an agreement with the Secretary to participate in the program for not less than a 3-year period (referred to in this section as the “agreement period”).

children.label: C
children.text: The ACO shall have a formal legal structure that would allow the organization to receive and distribute payments for shared savings under subsection (d)(2) to participating providers of services and suppliers.

children.label: D
children.text: The ACO shall include primary care ACO professionals that are sufficient for the number of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries assigned to the ACO under subsection (c). At a minimum, the ACO shall have at least 5,000 such beneficiaries assigned to it under subsection (c) in order to be eligible to participate in the ACO program.

children.label: E
children.text: The ACO shall provide the Secretary with such information regarding ACO professionals participating in the ACO as the Secretary determines necessary to support the assignment of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to an ACO, the implementation of quality and other reporting requirements under paragraph (3), and the determination of payments for shared savings under subsection (d)(2).

children.label: F
children.text: The ACO shall have in place a leadership and management structure that includes clinical and administrative systems.

children.label: G
children.text: The ACO shall define processes to promote evidence-based medicine and patient engagement, report on quality and cost measures, and coordinate care, such as through the use of telehealth, remote patient monitoring, and other such enabling technologies.

children.label: H
children.text: The ACO shall demonstrate to the Secretary that it meets patient-centeredness criteria specified by the Secretary, such as the use of patient and caregiver assessments or the use of individualized care plans.

children.label: I
children.text: An ACO that seeks to operate an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program pursuant to subsection (m) shall apply to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and with such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: clinical processes and outcomes;

children.label: ii
children.text: patient and, where practicable, caregiver experience of care; and

children.label: iii
children.text: utilization (such as rates of hospital admissions for ambulatory care sensitive conditions).

children.heading: Reporting requirements
children.label: B Reporting requirements
children.text: An ACO shall submit data in a form and manner specified by the Secretary on measures the Secretary determines necessary for the ACO to report in order to evaluate the quality of care furnished by the ACO. Such data may include care transitions across health care settings, including hospital discharge planning and post-hospital discharge follow-up by ACO professionals, as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Quality performance standards
children.label: C Quality performance standards
children.text: The Secretary shall establish quality performance standards to assess the quality of care furnished by ACOs. The Secretary shall seek to improve the quality of care furnished by ACOs over time by specifying higher standards, new measures, or both for purposes of assessing such quality of care.

children.heading: Other reporting requirements
children.label: D Other reporting requirements
children.text: The Secretary may, as the Secretary determines appropriate, incorporate reporting requirements and incentive payments related to the physician quality reporting initiative (PQRI) under , including such requirements and such payments related to electronic prescribing, electronic health records, and other similar initiatives under , and may use alternative criteria than would otherwise apply under such section for determining whether to make such payments. The incentive payments described in the preceding sentence shall not be taken into consideration when calculating any payments otherwise made under subsection (d).section 1395w–4 of this titlesection 1395w–4 of this title

children.heading: No duplication in participation in shared savings programs
children.label: 4 No duplication in participation in shared savings programs
A
children.text: A model tested or expanded under that involves shared savings under this subchapter, or any other program or demonstration project that involves such shared savings.section 1315a of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The independence at home medical practice pilot program under .section 1395cc–5 of this title

children.heading: Assignment of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to ACOs
children.label: c Assignment of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to ACOs
A
children.text: in the case of performance years beginning on or after , primary care services provided under this subchapter by an ACO professional described in subsection (h)(1)(A); andApril 1, 2012

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of performance years beginning on or after , services provided under this subchapter by a Federally qualified health center or rural health clinic (as those terms are defined in ), as may be determined by the Secretary.January 1, 2019section 1395x(aa) of this title

children.heading: Choice of prospective assignment
children.label: A Choice of prospective assignment
children.text: For each agreement period (effective for agreements entered into or renewed on or after ), in the case where an ACO established under the program is in a Track that provides for the retrospective assignment of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to the ACO, the Secretary shall permit the ACO to choose to have Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries assigned prospectively, rather than retrospectively, to the ACO for an agreement period.January 1, 2020

children.heading: Assignment based on voluntary identification by medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries
children.label: B Assignment based on voluntary identification by medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For performance year 2018 and each subsequent performance year, if a system is available for electronic designation, the Secretary shall permit a Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary to voluntarily identify an ACO professional as the primary care provider of the beneficiary for purposes of assigning such beneficiary to an ACO, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: notified of their ability to make an identification described in clause (i); and

children.label: II
children.text: informed of the process by which they may make and change such identification.

children.heading: Superseding claims-based assignment
children.label: iii Superseding claims-based assignment
children.text: A voluntary identification by a Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary under this subparagraph shall supersede any claims-based assignment otherwise determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Payments and treatment of savings
children.label: d Payments and treatment of savings
i
children.text: the ACO meets quality performance standards established by the Secretary under subsection (b)(3); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the ACO meets the requirement under subparagraph (B)(i).

children.heading: Determining savings
children.label: i Determining savings
children.text: In each year of the agreement period, an ACO shall be eligible to receive payment for shared savings under paragraph (2) only if the estimated average per capita Medicare expenditures under the ACO for Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries for parts A and B services, adjusted for beneficiary characteristics, is at least the percent specified by the Secretary below the applicable benchmark under clause (ii). The Secretary shall determine the appropriate percent described in the preceding sentence to account for normal variation in expenditures under this subchapter, based upon the number of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries assigned to an ACO.

children.heading: Establish and update benchmark
children.label: ii Establish and update benchmark
children.text: The Secretary shall estimate a benchmark for each agreement period for each ACO using the most recent available 3 years of per-beneficiary expenditures for parts A and B services for Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries assigned to the ACO. Such benchmark shall be adjusted for beneficiary characteristics and such other factors as the Secretary determines appropriate and updated by the projected absolute amount of growth in national per capita expenditures for parts A and B services under the original Medicare fee-for-service program, as estimated by the Secretary. Such benchmark shall be reset at the start of each agreement period.

children.heading: Payments for shared savings
children.label: 2 Payments for shared savings
children.text: Subject to performance with respect to the quality performance standards established by the Secretary under subsection (b)(3), if an ACO meets the requirements under paragraph (1), a percent (as determined appropriate by the Secretary) of the difference between such estimated average per capita Medicare expenditures in a year, adjusted for beneficiary characteristics, under the ACO and such benchmark for the ACO may be paid to the ACO as shared savings and the remainder of such difference shall be retained by the program under this subchapter. The Secretary shall establish limits on the total amount of shared savings that may be paid to an ACO under this paragraph.

children.heading: Monitoring avoidance of at-risk patients
children.label: 3 Monitoring avoidance of at-risk patients
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an ACO has taken steps to avoid patients at risk in order to reduce the likelihood of increasing costs to the ACO the Secretary may impose an appropriate sanction on the ACO, including termination from the program.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 4 Termination
children.text: The Secretary may terminate an agreement with an ACO if it does not meet the quality performance standards established by the Secretary under subsection (b)(3).

children.heading: Administration
children.label: e Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to the program, including an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program under subsections (b)(2)(I) and (m).

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: f Waiver authority
children.text: The Secretary may waive such requirements of sections 1320a–7a and 1320a–7b of this title and this subchapter as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.
the specification of criteria under subsection (a)(1)(B);
the assessment of the quality of care furnished by an ACO and the establishment of performance standards under subsection (b)(3);
the assignment of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to an ACO under subsection (c);

the determination of whether an ACO is eligible for shared savings under subsection (d)(2) and the amount of such shared savings, including the determination of the estimated average per capita Medicare expenditures under the ACO for Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries assigned to the ACO and the average benchmark for the ACO under subsection (d)(1)(B);
the percent of shared savings specified by the Secretary under subsection (d)(2) and any limit on the total amount of shared savings established by the Secretary under such subsection; and
the termination of an ACO under subsection (d)(4) or of an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program under subsections (b)(2)(I) and (m).

children.label: A
children.text: a physician (as defined in ); andsection 1395x(r)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a practitioner described in .section 1395u(b)(18)(C)(i) of this title

children.heading: Hospital
children.label: 2 Hospital
children.text: The term “hospital” means a subsection (d) hospital (as defined in ).section 1395ww(d)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary
children.label: 3 Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary
children.text: The term “Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary” means an individual who is enrolled in the original Medicare fee-for-service program under parts A and B and is not enrolled in an MA plan under part C, an eligible organization under , or a PACE program under .section 1395mm of this titlesection 1395eee of this title

children.heading: Option to use other payment models
children.label: i Option to use other payment models

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines appropriate, the Secretary may use any of the payment models described in paragraph (2) or (3) for making payments under the program rather than the payment model described in subsection (d).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), a model described in this paragraph is a partial capitation model in which an ACO is at financial risk for some, but not all, of the items and services covered under parts A and B, such as at risk for some or all physicians’ services or all items and services under part B. The Secretary may limit a partial capitation model to ACOs that are highly integrated systems of care and to ACOs capable of bearing risk, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: No additional program expenditures
children.label: B No additional program expenditures
children.text: Payments to an ACO for items and services under this subchapter for beneficiaries for a year under the partial capitation model shall be established in a manner that does not result in spending more for such ACO for such beneficiaries than would otherwise be expended for such ACO for such beneficiaries for such year if the model were not implemented, as estimated by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), a model described in this paragraph is any payment model that the Secretary determines will improve the quality and efficiency of items and services furnished under this subchapter.

children.heading: No additional program expenditures
children.label: B No additional program expenditures
children.text: Subparagraph (B) of paragraph (2) shall apply to a payment model under subparagraph (A) in a similar manner as such subparagraph (B) applies to the payment model under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Involvement in private payer and other third party arrangements
children.label: j Involvement in private payer and other third party arrangements
children.text: The Secretary may give preference to ACOs who are participating in similar arrangements with other payers.

children.heading: Treatment of physician group practice demonstration
children.label: k Treatment of physician group practice demonstration
children.text: During the period beginning on , and ending on the date the program is established, the Secretary may enter into an agreement with an ACO under the demonstration under , subject to rebasing and other modifications deemed appropriate by the Secretary.March 23, 2010section 1395cc–1 of this title

children.heading: Providing ACOs the ability to expand the use of telehealth services
children.label: l Providing ACOs the ability to expand the use of telehealth services

children.heading: Inclusion of home as originating site
children.label: A Inclusion of home as originating site
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), the home of a beneficiary shall be treated as an originating site described in .section 1395m(m)(4)(C)(ii) of this title

children.heading: No application of geographic limitation
children.label: B No application of geographic limitation
children.text: The geographic limitation under shall not apply with respect to an originating site described in (including the home of a beneficiary under subparagraph (A)), subject to State licensing requirements.section 1395m(m)(4)(C)(i) of this titlesection 1395m(m)(4)(C)(ii) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: described in section 425.600(a) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations; or

children.label: II
children.text: tested or expanded under ; andsection 1315a of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: for which Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries are assigned to the ACO using a prospective assignment method, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Home
children.label: B Home
children.text: The term “home” means, with respect to a Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary, the place of residence used as the home of the beneficiary.

children.heading: Telehealth services received in the home
children.label: 3 Telehealth services received in the home

children.heading: No facility fee
children.label: A No facility fee
children.text: There shall be no facility fee paid to the originating site under .section 1395m(m)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion of certain services
children.label: B Exclusion of certain services
children.text: No payment may be made for such services that are inappropriate to furnish in the home setting such as services that are typically furnished in inpatient settings such as a hospital.

children.heading: Authority to provide incentive payments to beneficiaries with respect to qualifying primary care services
children.label: m Authority to provide incentive payments to beneficiaries with respect to qualifying primary care services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In order to encourage Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to obtain medically necessary primary care services, an ACO participating under this section under a payment model described in clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (2)(B) may apply to establish an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program to provide incentive payments to such beneficiaries who are furnished qualifying services in accordance with this subsection. The Secretary shall permit such an ACO to establish such a program at the Secretary’s discretion and subject to such requirements, including program integrity requirements, as the Secretary determines necessary.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: B Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall implement this subsection on a date determined appropriate by the Secretary. Such date shall be no earlier than , and no later than .January 1, 2019January 1, 2020

children.heading: Duration
children.label: A Duration
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (H), an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program established under this subsection shall be conducted for such period (of not less than 1 year) as the Secretary may approve.

children.label: i
children.text: With respect to the Track 2 and Track 3 payment models described in section 425.600(a) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations (or in any successor regulation), Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries who are preliminarily prospectively or prospectively assigned (or otherwise assigned, as determined by the Secretary) to the ACO.

children.label: ii
children.text: With respect to any future payment models involving two-sided risk, Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries who are assigned to the ACO, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: an ACO professional described in subsection (h)(1)(A) who has a primary care specialty designation included in the definition of primary care physician under section 425.20 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation);

children.label: ii
children.text: an ACO professional described in subsection (h)(1)(B); or

children.label: iii
children.text: a Federally qualified health center or rural health clinic (as such terms are defined in ).section 1395x(aa) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in an amount up to $20, with such maximum amount updated annually by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the same amount for each Medicare fee-for-service beneficiary described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (B) without regard to enrollment of such a beneficiary in a medicare supplemental policy (described in ), in a State Medicaid plan under subchapter XIX or a waiver of such a plan, or in any other health insurance policy or health benefit plan;section 1395ss(g)(1) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: made for each qualifying service furnished to such a beneficiary described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (B) during a period specified by the Secretary; and

children.label: iv
children.text: made no later than 30 days after a qualifying service is furnished to such a beneficiary described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (B).

children.heading: No separate payments from the Secretary
children.label: E No separate payments from the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall not make any separate payment to an ACO for the costs, including incentive payments, of carrying out an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program established under this subsection. Nothing in this subparagraph shall be construed as prohibiting an ACO from using shared savings received under this section to carry out an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program.

children.heading: No application to shared savings calculation
children.label: F No application to shared savings calculation
children.text: Incentive payments made by an ACO under this subsection shall be disregarded for purposes of calculating benchmarks, estimated average per capita Medicare expenditures, and shared savings under this section.

children.heading: Reporting requirements
children.label: G Reporting requirements
children.text: An ACO conducting an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program under this subsection shall, at such times and in such format as the Secretary may require, report to the Secretary such information and retain such documentation as the Secretary may require, including the amount and frequency of incentive payments made and the number of Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries receiving such payments.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: H Termination
children.text: The Secretary may terminate an ACO Beneficiary Incentive Program established under this subsection at any time for reasons determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: determining eligibility for benefits or assistance (or the amount or extent of benefits or assistance) under any Federal program or under any State or local program financed in whole or in part with Federal funds; or

children.label: B
children.text: any Federal or State laws relating to taxation.
